author: Agent C

source.doc_status: originals not yet recovered into data/raw/process_docs/; methods authored from briefing extract.

ratification_legend.ratified_true: Method is source-owner sign-off complete; tools may compute and post.
ratification_legend.ratified_false: Method is documented but not approved; tools must refuse to compute and emit refusal_reason.

methods.name: Land Allocation at MDA Execution

calculation.conflict_resolution_note: Workbook inspection (2026-05-11, Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 CSV exports — Instructions tab + Allocation Engine columns) confirms sales-basis weighting is the observed allocation method. The earlier briefing/ontology lot-count formula is preserved below as a control / tie-out interpretation, NOT the workbook's allocation formula.
calculation.reconciliation_note: Lot counts remain required for the MDA Day tie-out gate and the per-lot denominator. Lot counts also drive Total Projected Sales (Total Projected Sales = Lots × Avg Projected Sales Price, per the Instructions tab). Sales Basis % per community is the actual allocation weighting. Both inputs are needed: lot counts for control/per-lot math; lots × pricing for the weighting.

sales_basis_weighted.source: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 CSV exports — Allocation Engine sheet columns 'Total Proj Sales', 'Sales Basis % (Community)', 'Community Land Pool', 'Land Allocated'; Instructions tab explicitly: 'Total Projected Sales = Lots × Price — this drives the allocation basis' and 'allocates each community indirect/land pool to its lot-type rows by projected-sales-basis %.'

lot_count_weighted_control_interpretatio.role: control / tie-out interpretation — useful as a sanity check on sales-basis allocation when pricing data is sparse, NOT the workbook's allocation formula

calculation.weighting_note: Lot-type weighting (SFR vs TH vs MF basis differential) is NOT modeled here; that would be a v2 method.

methods.verification_note: Workbook inspection (2026-05-11) confirms the Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 uses sales-basis weighting (Instructions tab + Allocation Engine columns). The lot-count formula from the briefing/ontology is preserved as a control/tie-out interpretation, not the workbook method. Formal source-owner ratification of sales-basis as canonical is still pending (Q23). Tools should present sales-basis as the current working method, surface the lot-count form as control only, and continue to block computation until pricing inputs are populated.

methods.name: Water Allocation per Water Requirement Letter
methods.verification_note: Briefing describes water allocating 'by letter' — interpreted as ratio of phase units to total units. Exact unit definition (acre-feet, taps, ERUs?) needs confirmation.

calculation.weighting_note: Equal-weight per lot within phase per briefing. Lot-type cost differential (e.g., SFR vs TH foundation cost) is not modeled in v0.2.

methods.name: Indirect Cost Allocation across Community by Sales Basis
methods.verification_note: Briefing notes 'sales_basis_pct OR lot_count_pct' — sales basis is preferred per Flagship workbook v3 pattern. Open question: does the choice of weighting need to be ratified per community?

methods.name: Warranty Accrual at Lot Sale
methods.verification_note: Default warranty rate not given in briefing. Open question: is the rate global, per DevCo, or per community?

methods.name: Range-Row / Shell Allocation (UNRATIFIED — refuse)

calculation.reason: Method not ratified by source owner. Holds approximately $45.75M / 4,020 rows at project+phase grain. Splitting to per-lot grain without source-owner sign-off would fabricate confidence.

methods.refusal_reason: Range-row allocation method is unratified. Pending source-owner Q6 sign-off before tools may compute per-lot allocations for range rows.
methods.verification_note: v2.1 BCPD operating-state work explicitly held this at project+phase grain. v0.2 inherits the same hold.

method_event_matrix.description: Quick lookup of which methods fire at which event.

open_questions: Default warranty rate value and scope (global vs DevCo vs community).
Water unit definition (acre-feet vs taps vs ERUs vs raw count).
Whether indirect allocation must use sales_basis or may fall back to lot_count without explicit per-community ratification.
Whether MPL (Master Planning) has its own allocation method or rolls into Indirect.
Whether lot-type weighting (SFR vs TH vs MF) modifies the equal-weight assumption in direct_per_phase.

Q23 (originated 2026-05-11 from MCP Desktop trial; narrowed 2026-05-11 after workbook CSV inspection): land_at_mda weighting — Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 CSV inspection (Instructions + Allocation Engine sheets) confirms sales-basis weighting is the observed workbook method. Lot-count formula from briefing/ontology is retained as a control/tie-out interpretation only. Formal source-owner ratification of sales-basis as canonical is still pending; once ratified, drop pending status and remove the lot_count_weighted_control_interpretation block.
